seoTitle: How to Create a Rich Filter in Jira Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to build a Rich Filter in Jira, connect it to saved filters, configure smart controls, and add it to your dashboards.
intro: Basic search filters can only get you so far when you're building an interactive dashboard. This guide covers creating a Rich Filter in Jira, connecting it to saved filters, and adding it to a dashboard for dynamic reporting.

steps.heading: Understand what a Rich Filter is
steps.body: A Rich Filter goes beyond a basic saved filter, offering smart controls that make dashboards more interactive and dynamic.

steps.heading: Create a new Rich Filter
steps.body: Start the setup process and give your filter a clear name describing what it's meant to track.

steps.heading: Configure smart filters
steps.body: Set up the smart filtering and controller options so the filter can respond dynamically as conditions change.

steps.heading: Add the Rich Filter to a dashboard
steps.body: Insert the finished filter as a gadget on your dashboard so your team can interact with it directly.

faq.question: What is a Rich Filter in Jira?
faq.answer: A Rich Filter is an advanced filter type with smart controls, built to support more interactive dashboards than a basic saved filter.

faq.question: How do I connect a Rich Filter to an existing saved filter?
faq.answer: During the setup process, link the new Rich Filter to a saved filter you already have so it builds on that query.

faq.question: Can I put a Rich Filter directly on my dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, once configured you can add it as a gadget to any dashboard you have permission to edit.

faq.question: Who benefits most from using Rich Filters?
faq.answer: Jira administrators, project managers, and Scrum Masters often use Rich Filters to build clearer, more interactive reporting views.
